title: rosa tomentosa var. annesiensis
intro: Rosa tomentosa var. annesiensis, also known as the hairy rose, is a plant species within the Rosa genus. It is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, known for its hairy leaves and stems. The hairy rose has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
